description: Phase 9 — Offline First. Service worker via vite-plugin-pwa, IndexedDB caching of beaches and forecasts, network-first fetch with fallback, offline banner, and prompt-to-update banner.

userStories.title: Install vite-plugin-pwa and configure service worker
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service worker registered so the app shell is cached and the app loads offline.
acceptanceCriteria: vite-plugin-pwa installed via yarn add -D vite-plugin-pwa in ui/
vite.config.ts updated: import VitePWA from vite-plugin-pwa and add to plugins array
PWA config: registerType 'prompt', workbox.globPatterns ['**/*.{js,css,html,ico,png,svg,woff2}']
manifest section with name 'surfsapp', short_name 'surfsapp', theme_color '#65dfbf', background_color '#ffffff', display 'standalone'
Add type reference for vite-plugin-pwa/vue in env.d.ts or vite-env.d.ts (/// <reference types='vite-plugin-pwa/vue' />) so virtual:pwa-register/vue imports typecheck
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT add icon files — Phase 10 handles icons. The manifest can reference placeholder paths. registerType 'prompt' means the new SW waits for user action before activating (US-006 builds the prompt UI). Service worker only works in production builds (yarn build && yarn preview), not dev mode.

userStories.title: Extend Dexie schema for beaches and forecasts cache
userStories.description: As a developer, I need IndexedDB tables to cache API responses so the app can serve data offline.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/db.ts updated: Dexie version bumped to 2
New table beachesCache with schema '&id' — stores full Beach objects
New table forecastsCache with schema '&beachId' — stores full ForecastSnapshot objects
TypeScript interfaces added: BeachCacheRecord (same shape as Beach) and ForecastCacheRecord (same shape as ForecastSnapshot)
Tables typed on the SurfsAppDB class: db.beachesCache and db.forecastsCache are fully typed
Existing v1 tables (favourites, settings) preserved in the v2 upgrade — v1 stores definition repeated in v2
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dexie requires repeating all stores in each version declaration. Import Beach and ForecastSnapshot types from types/beach.ts.

userStories.title: Cache beaches and forecasts on successful fetch
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to save the latest data locally so it's available when I'm offline.
acceptanceCriteria: In beachStore.fetchBeaches(): after successful API response and enrichment, write all beaches to db.beachesCache via db.beachesCache.bulkPut(enriched)
In beachStore.fetchBeaches(): for each beach where the forecast fetch succeeded, write the forecast to db.forecastsCache via db.forecastsCache.put(forecast) — the forecast object must include beachId
In beachStore.fetchBeachDetail(id): after successful API response, write beach to db.beachesCache.put(beach) and forecast to db.forecastsCache.put(forecast)
All IndexedDB writes are fire-and-forget (.catch(console.error)) — never block UI on cache writes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The forecast object from GET /beaches/:id/forecast already contains beachId. For the listing enrichment, you need to store the raw forecast response before extracting swell/wind fields — capture it in the per-beach map callback.

userStories.title: Network-first fetch with IndexedDB fallback
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to show cached data when I'm offline so I can still check conditions.
acceptanceCriteria: In beachStore.fetchBeaches(): wrap existing network fetch in try/catch; on network failure, read from db.beachesCache.toArray() and for each cached beach read its forecast from db.forecastsCache.get(beach.id); enrich cached beaches the same way as network beaches (swell/wind/bestWindow extraction from forecast); set this.beaches from cache
If both network and cache fail (no cached data), set this.error to 'No internet connection and no cached data available'
In beachStore.fetchBeachDetail(id): on network failure, read from db.beachesCache.get(id) and db.forecastsCache.get(id); set selectedBeach and selectedForecast from cache
If detail cache miss, set detailError to 'No internet connection and no cached data for this beach'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The enrichment logic (extracting swell/wind/bestWindow from forecast hourlyForecasts and dailySummaries) must work identically for both network and cached data. Consider extracting a helper function to avoid duplication.

userStories.title: Offline banner
userStories.description: As a user, I want to know when I'm offline so I understand the data may be stale.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/composables/useOnlineStatus.ts created: exports a reactive isOnline ref tracking navigator.onLine, listens to window online/offline events, cleans up listeners on scope dispose
App.vue imports useOnlineStatus and renders a top banner when !isOnline
Banner has amber/yellow background (--color-surf-maybe or #f59e0b), white bold text, full-width, fixed to top with z-index above header
Banner text: 'You're offline — showing cached data'
Banner has a slide-down CSS transition when appearing (~200ms) and slide-up when disappearing
When banner is visible, page content shifts down (not overlapped) — use a top padding/margin on the app container equal to the banner height
Banner disappears immediately when connectivity returns
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: navigator.onLine is not 100% reliable (captive portals) but is sufficient for MVP. Use onScopeDispose from Vue to clean up event listeners.

userStories.title: Prompt-to-update banner for new service worker
userStories.description: As a user, I want to know when a new version of the app is available so I can update.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/composables/useServiceWorker.ts created: imports useRegisterSW from virtual:pwa-register/vue, exposes needRefresh (boolean ref) and updateServiceWorker (function)
App.vue imports useServiceWorker and renders a top banner when needRefresh is true
Banner has blue/primary background, white text: 'New version available' with a <surf-button> labelled 'Refresh' that calls updateServiceWorker(true)
If both offline banner and update banner would show simultaneously, offline banner takes priority (update banner hidden)
Banner has the same slide-down/slide-up animation style as the offline banner
Page content shifts down when banner is visible
After clicking Refresh, the page reloads with the new service worker active
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: virtual:pwa-register/vue is provided by vite-plugin-pwa and requires registerType 'prompt' (set in US-001). This composable only works in production builds — in dev mode needRefresh will always be false. Ensure the type reference from US-001 is in place so the import typechecks.
